# Generation Lab Raises $11M Seed Round as SystemAge™ Becomes World’s Fastest-Growing Longevity Diagnostic

• By Generation Lab

##### *Among the top 1% of startup raises, the round will accelerate adoption of the SystemAge breakthrough aging diagnostic platform across 275+ clinics and tens of thousands of people worldwide*

**San Francisco, Calif., October 23, 2025** — [Generation Lab](http://www.generationlab.com), the company delivering breakthroughs in longevity science, today announced the completion of an $11 million seed funding round, bringing total funding to $15 million and placing the company in the top 1% of all startup seed raises. Led by Accel, the round also includes notable investors Samsung Next, Zone2, EDM legend Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo's Build Your Legacy (BYL) Ventures, and actor Simu Liu's Markham Valley Ventures.

Less than ten months post-launch, Generation Lab has achieved remarkable commercial traction with its SystemAge™ quantification of biological age—health of 19 organ systems/functions from intact primary blood results—that is based on discovery of the physiologic non-linear curve of DNA methylation aging. SystemAge greatly improves on alternatives that make predictions from man-made graphs after transforming data in a biologically arbitrary way. In less than a year of operation, the company has signed over 275 clinics worldwide, amassed more than 300 million human-aging data points, and achieved 99.9% diagnostic accuracy, providing for the first time a clinically relevant capacity for meaningful, well-controlled healthspan optimization—the next frontier in global and personalized medicine.

**Market Leadership Through Clinical Adoption**

SystemAge™ has rapidly gained adoption across diverse healthcare settings, from leading academic medical centers to boutique wellness practices. The platform is trusted by renowned longevity physicians, professional athletes, and celebrities, with early advocates including leading longevity experts Professors David Sinclair and George Church of Harvard Medical School, Dr. Adeel Khan, award-winning author Dr. Michael Roizen, and Aubrey de Grey.

"We're witnessing a fundamental shift from reactive to proactive healthcare—led by consumers," said Alina Su, CEO and Co-founder of Generation Lab. "Our explosive growth, from zero to 275+ clinic partners in under a year, demonstrates that both clinicians and consumers are hungry for actionable aging insights. This funding allows us to accelerate our mission of making longevity diagnostics the standard of care globally. We're not just measuring aging; we're creating the roadmap to reverse it."

"Generation Lab is shaping the future of the longevity industry," said Kerry Wang, Partner at Accel. "Alina, Michael, and Irina are building with conviction and credibility in a space that demands both. Their SystemAge™ platform combines solid science with strong commercial progress, creating a new category in data-driven healthcare. This is only the beginning."

**Revolutionary Science Driving Results**

SystemAge™ measures the progression of biological aging across 19 distinct physiological systems (cardiovascular, reproductive, immune, fibrotic, metabolic, regenerative, etc.), with comprehensive mechanistic insights that enable effective personalized intervention strategies. The platform's breakthrough dataset has already established striking causality between SystemAge-found biological dysregulation—noise and disease-propensity—later confirmed in clinical tests that would not have been otherwise prescribed to pre-symptomatic individuals. In contrast to subjective so-called biological clocks that make correlative predictions from biologically arbitrary numbers with decades-large errors, SystemAge objectively and accurately quantifies changes critical for health from intact primary data. Interestingly, 70% of tested individuals were found to be biologically older than their chronological age, and sadly, company founders age 1.5 times faster than investors.

"What sets SystemAge™ apart is our systems-based approach to aging measurement using what we're calling 'biological noise,'" explained Dr. Irina Conboy, Co-founder of Generation Lab and renowned aging researcher at UC Berkeley. "While other platforms offer disease biomarkers, we've developed the first preventive health test detecting signals before symptoms appear. This is the most comprehensive aging diagnostic, analyzing how multiple organ systems age in concert. Our proprietary algorithms, trained on hundreds of millions of data points, don't just tell you how old you are—they reveal which specific systems are aging fastest and provide precise interventions to reverse the process."

**Proven Clinical Impact**

Real-world clinical data demonstrates SystemAge™'s ability to guide effective interventions. Clinical studies of SystemAge before versus after MUSE Stemcell treatment have shown 13.6 years of brain age reversal and 4.9 years of reproductive system age reversal. Anti-inflammatory diets and targeted fasting protocols can measurably slow organ aging, while optimized exercise regimens can reverse heart and immune system biological age by multiple years within six months.

The platform is detecting aging signals before symptoms appear, enabling truly preventative care pathways that could transform healthcare economics and patient outcomes.

**About Generation Lab**

Founded by renowned aging researcher Dr. Irina Conboy, Generation Lab is pioneering the future of longevity diagnostics through its flagship SystemAge™ platform. The company's mission is to create the Ageless Generation by providing the most comprehensive, accurate, and actionable aging diagnostics available. Based in San Francisco, Generation Lab serves over 275 clinic partners worldwide. For more information, visit [www.generationlab.com](http://www.generationlab.com).

‍

**Media Contact:**  
Alex Plant  
Generation Lab  
alex@generationlab.com

‍

![Meta Pixel](https://www.facebook.com/tr?id=724051843444432&ev=PageView&noscript=1)

# Can You Reverse Biological Age? Science-Backed Methods

Your driver's license says you're 45, but do you feel it? Some days you feel 35, others 55. This isn't just a feeling; it is a reflection of a deeper truth about aging that science is beginning to understand.

While we think of age as the birthday candles, there's another age that matters more: your biological age. This is how old your cells and organs *are,* independent of your years alive. Unlike your chronological age, your biological age can change.

Can you reverse your biological age? According to research from UC Berkeley and Harvard, yes. First, we need to understand biological age and why measuring it precisely is the first step toward controlling your longevity journey.

## Why Your Biological Age Matters

Chronological age is the fixed number of years you've been alive since birth. It's a measure of time passing, like a clock that never stops ticking. While useful for legal documents and birthday celebrations, it tells us nothing about your health or vitality.

Biological age is a dynamic measure of your body's health and cellular condition. It is like comparing two cars. Both might be 2015 models (same chronological age), but the one with 200,000 hard highway miles is in worse condition than the one with 30,000 gently-driven city miles. The wear and tear is biological age.

You can't change your chronological age, but your biological age is influenced by genetics, lifestyle, environment, and chosen interventions. What you can influence, you can improve and even reverse.

A biological age test is crucial. Without precise measurement, you're driving blind on your health journey, making decisions based on how you feel rather than the cellular level.

## Your Epigenetics: Science of Aging

To understand how to reverse biological age, we need to explore epigenetics, which is the science revolutionizing our understanding of aging and health.

### What is Epigenetics?

If DNA is your body's hardware, which is the permanent blueprint containing all your genetic information, then epigenetics is the software that tells your genes which ones to turn on or off. Think of genes like light bulbs in a house, and epigenetics as the dimmer switches controlling their brightness.

Epigenetic "switches" don't change your DNA sequence, but they influence gene expression. They determine whether a gene for cellular repair is activated or silenced, whether inflammatory pathways are triggered, or whether cells maintain their youthful function. This is why identical twins with the same DNA can age differently because their epigenetic patterns diverge based on life experiences.

### DNA Methylation: Measuring Biological Age

The primary mechanism of epigenetic control is DNA methylation. Picture tiny chemical tags on your DNA acting like Post-it notes telling your cells which genetic instructions to follow. When you're young and healthy, these methylation patterns are organized and purposeful.

As we age or face stress, pollution, poor nutrition, and other challenges, these patterns become chaotic. This creates "biomolecular noise", which are random signals that interfere with your cells' optimal function. It's like static on a radio, making it harder for your cells to receive clear instructions.

Epigenetic age testing is revolutionary. Scientists can detect biomolecular noise years before symptoms or disease by analyzing DNA methylation patterns. SystemAge is the only test that acts as a 'DNA methylation noise detector,' identifying subtle signals of aging and disease risk with precision.

### The Need for Organ-Specific Aging

Here's where most biological age tests fall short: they give you a single number, like getting one grade for an entire school year. But your body doesn't age uniformly. Your heart might be aging faster than your brain. Your immune system could be functioning like someone 10 years younger, while your liver shows signs of accelerated aging.

This organ-specific aging pattern is crucial because it tells you where to focus your efforts. Knowing your overall biological age is 40 doesn't help you decide whether to prioritize cardiovascular exercise, cognitive training, or immune system support. But knowing that your heart age is 48 while your brain age is 35 gives you a clear action plan.

To take targeted action against aging, you need organ-level data. This granular approach separates clinical-grade testing from consumer products.

## The Answer: Yes, You Can Reverse Your Biological Age

Can you reverse your biological age? The scientific consensus, backed by over two decades of research, is yes. This isn't wishful thinking or marketing hype; it's established science published in *Nature*, *Aging*, and *GeroScience*.

[Dr. Irina Conboy](https://www.generationlab.com/science), co-founder of Generation Lab and renowned UC Berkeley professor, called the 'mother of longevity,' is leading this scientific revolution. Her work in plasma dilution proved that the aging process could be reversed at a molecular level, demonstrating that old cells could regain youthful function under the right conditions. This research, along with contributions from Harvard and other institutions, forms the foundation of our understanding that aging isn't irreversible.

### The First Step to Reversal is Precise Measurement

You can't manage what you don't measure. To reverse biological aging, you need to know your starting point. This means not just one number, but a detailed map of your body's aging patterns.

Think of it like improving your financial health. Would you rather know "your finances need work" or receive a detailed breakdown of your housing, food, entertainment, and investment spending? The specific data empowers action.

## Best Biological Age Test: System Age

SystemAge is the first and only clinical-grade, at-home epigenetic test that analyzes over 460 DNA methylation biomarkers to measure your biological age across 19 organ systems. You can access laboratory-grade precision from home using a simple, needle-free blood collection device.

This isn't just another consumer health test. It's the same technology used in 150+ longevity clinics worldwide to track patient outcomes and optimize treatment protocols.

Why SystemAge is the Gold Standard in Biological Age Testing:

### SystemAge Comparison

SystemAge provides the most comprehensive, organ-specific epigenetic analysis, while other tests like TruDiagnostic offer fewer organ scores (~11) with lower accuracy. TruDiagnostic's clock also relies on linear regression — a straightforward statistical technique that assumes simple, proportional relationships between CpG sites and aging, overlooking the nonlinear, systems-level dynamics of how we actually age. Without prospective clinical evidence that its output predicts disease or mortality, the number it generates remains a statistical artifact rather than a validated health measure. Function Health uses standard blood markers that can't detect 'biomolecular noise,' and cheek-swab tests (Tally Health) lack the clinical accuracy of SystemAge's blood-based method for detecting subtle cellular changes that predict future health outcomes.

## Real-World Results: Tracking Measurable Age Reversal

In clinical settings, SystemAge is used to track intervention efficacy, with documented results proving measurable biological age reversal:

These cases represent growing evidence that significant biological age reversal is achievable with the right interventions and precise measurement tools.

### Trusted by 150+ Clinics and Longevity Experts

SystemAge is the diagnostic tool of choice for over 150 longevity clinics worldwide. Practitioners demanding high standards of accuracy and clinical utility choose it.

"Having organ-specific biological ages allows us to create personalized treatment protocols. Instead of generic anti-aging approaches, we can target where each patient needs the most support."

"Dr. Adeel Khan, whose clinic has documented numerous cases of measurable age reversal using SystemAge tracking, adds, 'The precision of SystemAge is remarkable. We can now measure the biological impact of our interventions, adjusting protocols based on real data rather than subjective patient reports.'"

Even Harvard genetics pioneer Dr. George Church recognizes the breakthrough nature of this technology, noting its potential to transform preventive healthcare and longevity medicine.

## Your Longevity Journey Starts Now: The 3-Step Process

Controlling your biological age is simpler than you think. Here's how to get started:

### Step 1: Get Your Baseline from Home

Order your SystemAge kit online and receive it within days. Using the included needle-free blood collection device, collect a small blood sample at home with no clinic visits, no painful needles, no scheduling hassles. Send it back using the pre-paid shipper, and you're done.

### Step 2: Receive Your Personalized Action Plan

In 3-4 weeks, you'll receive a digital report detailing your biological age across 19 organ systems, along with science-backed recommendations tailored to your aging pattern. The recommendations will include targeted diet modifications, supplement protocols, exercise routines, and lifestyle changes prioritized by which organ systems need the most attention.

### Step 3: Implement, Track, and Verify Progress

This is where the real transformation begins. Implement your personalized recommendations and re-test periodically to measure your progress. SystemAge acts as your health GPS, helping you navigate toward optimal aging by showing what's working and what needs adjustment. The "measure, intervene, verify" loop ensures you make real, quantifiable progress rather than hoping for the best.

## Conclusion

Your chronological age is just a number, which is an arbitrary marker of time's passage. But your biological age is the true measure of your health, vitality, and longevity potential. Science has proven you can reverse your biological age with the right approach.

The key is precision. Stop guessing about your health and start measuring. This is about prevention over treatment, empowerment through knowledge, and turning aging into a game you can play and win. Every intervention you make, including the foods you eat to the supplements you take to the exercise routines you follow, can be measured for its biological impact.

The future of healthcare isn't about treating diseases after they appear; it's about detecting and preventing them years before symptoms develop. SystemAge puts that power in your hands, giving you the same clinical-grade tools used by the world's leading longevity experts.

## FAQs

**Q: How to measure biological age at home with SystemAge?**

A: SystemAge uses a simple, needle-free blood collection device for home use. The process takes minutes, and you send your sample back using our pre-paid shipping kit.

**Q: How accurate is the SystemAge test?**

A: SystemAge has a clinical-grade accuracy rate in detecting biological age changes. It has been validated across 2,000+ test cases, making it the most accurate test available to consumers.

**Q: Is the sample collection process painful?**

A: Not at all. SystemAge uses a painless, needle-free blood collection device that requires no medical training or clinic visits. It's designed for easy home use.

**Q: How long will it take to get my results?**

A: You’ll get your digital report and personalized action plan within 3-4 weeks of our lab receiving your sample.

**Q: What recommendations will I receive?**

A: Your report includes personalized, science-backed intervention plans for your highest-priority organ systems, with recommendations for diet, exercise, supplements, and lifestyle adjustments based on your unique aging pattern.

**Q: Can I reverse my biological age, or just slow it down?**

A: Clinical data shows true biological age reversal is possible. SystemAge documented cases of 13.6-year reductions in brain age, 10.4-year immune system reversals, and an average 5.5-year reduction across multiple organ systems with targeted interventions.

# GlycanAge Test Reviews: Is It a Legitimate?

If you're reading GlycanAge test reviews, you're asking: how old am I on the inside? This quest to understand biological age has led health-conscious individuals to explore testing options beyond traditional blood panels and physical exams. GlycanAge has gained popularity as a way to measure one aspect of aging: inflammation through the analysis of complex sugar molecules attached to immune proteins.

Does a single inflammation score capture the complexity of your body’s aging? Or is it like understanding a forest’s health by examining one tree? While inflammation is important, aging affects every organ system differently. Your heart, brain, immune system, and metabolism each have their own timeline.

This article examines what GlycanAge can tell you about your health, real user experiences, and a more comprehensive, next-generation approach to [understanding your biological age](https://www.generationlab.com/the-systemage-test) beyond a single inflammation marker.

## What is the GlycanAge Test?

To understand GlycanAge measurements, we need to start with glycans, which are complex sugar molecules that attach to proteins, particularly immunoglobulins (IgG), a type of antibody crucial for immune response. Glycans are tiny "switches" on your immune cells that can promote or calm inflammation. The structure of these glycans changes with age and lifestyle factors, creating a measurable signature of your inflammatory state.

The connection between glycans, inflammation, and aging centers on "inflammaging," which is chronic, low-grade inflammation driving aging. As we age, our IgG glycans shift toward a more pro-inflammatory state. This isn't necessarily disease, but a gradual change in our immune system. Young, healthy individuals have glycan patterns favoring anti-inflammatory responses, while older adults show patterns associated with increased inflammation.

The GlycanAge test uses a simple at-home blood sample collected via finger prick, which you send to their lab for analysis. They analyze the glycans attached to your IgG antibodies using advanced glycomics technology. The result is a single "GlycanAge" score that compares your inflammatory profile to population averages for your chronological age. A higher GlycanAge suggests a more pro-inflammatory state, while a lower score indicates an inflammatory profile typical of younger individuals.

## GlycanAge Test Reviews: What Are Users Saying?

Analyzing public GlycanAge test reviews reveals a mixed but generally positive response. Users appreciate certain aspects and identify limitations. Many users find value in the test's focused approach to measuring inflammation but want more comprehensive health insights.

### The Positives: Why People Like the GlycanAge Test

### Limitations: Common Criticisms and Gaps

## GlycanAge Test Review: Verdict

Monitoring inflammation is crucial to any longevity strategy. Chronic inflammation contributes to almost every age-related disease, from cardiovascular issues to cognitive decline. GlycanAge serves as a legitimate biomarker for tracking one aspect of healthy aging.

Aging is a multi-faceted process affecting every organ system differently and at different rates. Your cardiovascular system might age rapidly while your immune system remains robust, or your brain health might decline while your metabolic function stays strong. Relying solely on an inflammation score to assess your biological age is like a CEO judging their company's performance based only on the marketing budget because it misses the bigger picture of research and development, operations, finance, and customer service.

This single-biomarker approach to biological age testing leaves critical blind spots in your health assessment. You need a system that sees the whole picture and not just one part to optimize your healthspan. The science of epigenetics offers a revolutionary leap in measuring biological age.

## Top Alternative to the GlycanAge Test

If your DNA is the hardware of your body, then epigenetics is the software that tells your genes which to turn on or off. The key mechanism of epigenetic regulation, DNA methylation, acts like a control system that determines how your genes function throughout your life. This epigenetic "software" doesn't change your genetic code, but it influences how those genes are expressed.

As we age, epigenetic software can become "noisy" or disorganized, leading to suboptimal gene function and increased disease risk. The most sensitive, earliest signal of aging is methylation noise, which appears years before traditional biomarkers or clinical symptoms. By measuring specific DNA methylation patterns across hundreds of genome sites, scientists create accurate biological age assessments.

Generation Lab is at the forefront of the epigenetic revolution. Co-founded by renowned UC Berkeley professor Dr. Irina Conboy, known as the "mother of longevity," Generation Lab is built on 20+ years of research from UC Berkeley and Harvard. This foundation, combined with cutting-edge laboratory capabilities, led to the SystemAge test, which is the most advanced epigenetic age testing platform available directly to consumers.

## Conclusion

After analyzing GlycanAge test reviews and the science behind biological age tests, the conclusion is clear: GlycanAge is a valid tool for measuring inflammation, but true longevity optimization requires a comprehensive approach. Knowing your inflammatory status is valuable, but it is only one piece of a complex aging puzzle.

SystemAge offers a high-resolution, 19-system map of your biological age, backed by Ivy League science and decades of peer-reviewed research. It provides actionable, personalized guidance to optimize each system based on your aging patterns. This isn't just another health test; it is your GPS for navigating the aging process.

Stop guessing about your health and hoping generic wellness advice works for your biology. It's time to turn aging into a challenge you can master with precision and confidence. With the right data and targeted interventions, you can control your biological destiny.

# Can You Reverse Aging? The Scientific Consensus

Can you reverse aging? This question, once relegated to science fiction, is now at the forefront of serious research and breakthroughs. The answer is more complex and more hopeful than a simple yes or no.

Here's the key insight revolutionizing our understanding of aging: there's a crucial difference between your chronological age (the number of candles on your birthday cake) and your biological age (how your body is aging at the cellular level). While you can't turn back your chronological clock, breakthrough science shows you *can* influence and even reverse your biological age. This represents the new frontier of preventive health.

This article explores the scientific consensus on aging and introduces the technology for measuring and managing biological age. This breakthrough is achieved through epigenetic age testing, pioneered by scientists at Harvard and UC Berkeley. [Generation Lab is bringing this clinical-grade technology](https://www.generationlab.com/) from top research laboratories to your home.

The implications are profound. For the first time in human history, we can measure aging precisely, understand it at the molecular level, and take targeted action to slow or even reverse the aging process in specific organs and systems throughout your body.

## Biological Age and Epigenetics: What It Is and How It Works

Your biological age reveals how your body is aging. Two people born on the same day can have different biological ages based on genetics, lifestyle, environment, and health. One might have the cellular profile of someone 10 years younger, while the other shows signs of accelerated aging that add years to their biological clock.

This distinction matters because biological age predicts healthspan, disease risk, and longevity better than chronological age. Traditional medicine treats a 50-year-old as "50," but precision medicine recognizes one might be biologically 42 and another 58. This difference determines cardiovascular risk, cognitive decline, immune function, and cancer susceptibility.

### A Simple Guide to Epigenetics

To understand how we measure biological age, think of your DNA as your body's "hardware," which is the fundamental code that makes you who you are. The "software" that tells this hardware what to do is epigenetics. These epigenetic marks act as molecular switches that can turn genes "on" or "off" without changing your DNA sequence.

As you age, experience stress, change your diet, exercise, or encounter environmental factors, epigenetic switches change in predictable patterns. Your dynamic epigenetic profile responds to your lifestyle and environment, unlike your static DNA. This is where age reversal becomes scientifically grounded. By influencing these patterns, you can change how your genes express themselves.

### DNA Methylation

DNA methylation is the most studied and accurate epigenetic mechanism for tracking aging. As we age, predictable methylation patterns accumulate across our genome like molecular fingerprints of time. Scientists describe this accumulation as "biomolecular noise," which is the drift away from our youthful epigenetic state.

Researchers can calculate your biological age precisely by measuring molecular noise across hundreds of specific DNA sites. This forms the foundation of the most accurate biological age test methods today. This approach is not just diagnostic; it is actionable. Since epigenetic patterns can change, tracking them allows you to measure the effectiveness of interventions designed to slow or reverse aging.

## Starting Your Journey to Reverse Aging

Generation Lab's SystemAge test represents over 20 years of research in aging and regenerative medicine. The company is led by co-founder UC Berkeley professor Dr. Irina Conboy, called the "mother of longevity" for her work in aging research. Dr. Conboy's laboratory invented therapeutic plasma exchange for biological age reversal and has published extensively in top-tier journals like *Nature* and *Cell*.

The company's mission is to democratize preventive healthcare by making clinical-grade testing from research institutions accessible to individuals wanting to control their health. SystemAge transforms complex laboratory science into actionable intelligence that empowers you to make informed decisions about your health and longevity.

Not all biological age tests are equal. Some provide a single biological age number, while SystemAge delivers unprecedented detail that transforms health management.

SystemAge is the most comprehensive **biological age test** available, providing detailed intelligence previously found only in research settings.

## How to Reverse Your Biological Age

Knowing your biological age is just the beginning. SystemAge doesn't just provide data; it delivers actionable intelligence that transforms knowledge into results. Each organ system's biological age comes with personalized intervention recommendations tailored to your aging patterns. If your cardiovascular system shows accelerated aging while your immune system appears youthful, your action plan reflects these differences.

This precision approach means you're not following generic anti-aging advice. Instead, you're implementing targeted interventions based on your unique biological profile. Whether through dietary modifications, targeted supplementation, exercise protocols, stress management techniques, or medical interventions, your personalized plan addresses your body's actual needs rather than assumptions based on your chronological age.
